records.ety: [OE. right, riht, AS. riht; akin to D. regt, OS. & OHG. reht, G. recht, Dan. ret, Sw. rätt, Icel. rettr, Goth. rahts, L. rectus, p. p. of regere to guide, rule; cf. Skr. ju straight, right. √115. Cf. Adroit,Alert, Correct, Dress, Regular, Rector, Recto, Rectum, Regent, Region, Realm, Rich, Royal, Rule.]

defs: Straight; direct; not crooked; as, a right line.
Upright; erect from a base; having an upright axis; not oblique; as, right ascension; a right pyramid or cone.
Conformed to the constitution of man and the will of God, or to justice and equity; not deviating from the true and just; according with truth and duty; just; true.
quotes: That which is conformable to the Supreme Rule is absolutely right, and is called right simply without relation to a special end.

defs: Fit; suitable; proper; correct; becoming; as, the right man in the right place; the right way from London to Oxford.
Characterized by reality or genuineness; real; actual; not spurious.
quotes: In this battle, . . . the Britons never more plainly manifested themselves to be right barbarians.

defs: According with truth; passing a true judgment; conforming to fact or intent; not mistaken or wrong; not erroneous; correct; as, this is the right faith.
quotes: You are right, Justice, and you weigh this well.
If there be no prospect beyond the grave, the inference is . . . right, “Let us eat and drink, for to-morrow we die.”

defs: Most favorable or convenient; fortunate.
quotes: The lady has been disappointed on the right side.

defs: Of or pertaining to that side of the body in man on which the muscular action is usually stronger than on the other side; -- opposed to left when used in reference to a part of the body; as, the right side, hand, arm. Also applied to the corresponding side of the lower animals.
quotes: Became the sovereign's favorite, his right hand.
notes: ☞ In designating the banks of a river, right and left are used always with reference to the position of one who is facing in the direction of the current's flow.

defs: Well placed, disposed, or adjusted; orderly; well regulated; correctly done.
Designed to be placed or worn outward; as, the right side of a piece of cloth.
quotes: “Right,” cries his lordship.
notes: ☞ Right is used elliptically for it is right, what you say is right, true.

defs: In a right manner.
In a right or straight line; directly; hence; straightway; immediately; next; as, he stood right before me; it went right to the mark; he came right out; he followed right after the guide.
quotes: Unto Dian's temple goeth she right.
Let thine eyes look right on.
Right across its track there lay, Down in the water, a long reef of gold.

defs: Exactly; just.
quotes: Came he right now to sing a raven's note?

defs: According to the law or will of God; conforming to the standard of truth and justice; righteously; as, to live right; to judge right.
According to any rule of art; correctly.
quotes: You with strict discipline instructed right.

defs: According to fact or truth; actually; truly; really; correctly; exactly; as, to tell a story right.
quotes: Right as it were a steed of Lumbardye.
His wounds so smarted that he slept right naught.

defs: In a great degree; very; wholly; unqualifiedly; extremely; highly; as, right humble; right noble; right valiant.
quotes: For which I should be right sorry.
[I] return those duties back as are right fit.
notes: ☞ In this sense now chiefly prefixed to titles; as, right honorable; right reverend.
☞ Right is used in composition with other adverbs, as upright, downright, forthright, etc.

defs: That which is right or correct.
The straight course; adherence to duty; obedience to lawful authority, divine or human; freedom from guilt, -- the opposite of moral wrong.
A true statement; freedom from error of falsehood; adherence to truth or fact.
A just judgment or action; that which is true or proper; justice; uprightness; integrity.
quotes: Seldom your opinions err; Your eyes are always in the right.
Long love to her has borne the faithful knight, And well deserved, had fortune done him right.

defs: That to which one has a just claim.
That which one has a natural claim to exact.
That which one has a legal or social claim to do or to exact; legal power; authority; as, a sheriff has a right to arrest a criminal.
That which justly belongs to one; that which one has a claim to possess or own; the interest or share which anyone has in a piece of property; title; claim; interest; ownership.
Privilege or immunity granted by authority.
quotes: There are no rights whatever, without corresponding duties.
Born free, he sought his right.
Hast thou not right to all created things?
Men have no right to what is not reasonable.

defs: The right side; the side opposite to the left.
quotes: Led her to the Souldan's right.

defs: In some legislative bodies of Europe (as in France), those members collectively who are conservatives or monarchists. See Center, 5.
The outward or most finished surface, as of a piece of cloth, a carpet, etc.
quotes: He should himself use it by right.
I should have been a woman by right.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Righted; p. pr. & vb. n. Righting.]

defs: To bring or restore to the proper or natural position; to set upright; to make right or straight (that which has been wrong or crooked); to correct.
To do justice to; to relieve from wrong; to restore rights to; to assert or regain the rights of; as, to right the oppressed; to right one's self; also, to vindicate.
quotes: So just is God, to right the innocent.
All experience hath shown that mankind are more disposed to suffer while evils are sufferable, than to right themselves by abolishing the forms to which they are accustomed.

defs: To recover the proper or natural condition or position; to become upright.
Hence, to regain an upright position, as a ship or boat, after careening.
